commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer selection among related candidate PRs; there is no narrow automated repair left in this review.; Cleared: The diff changes gateway lifecycle code and tests only; I found no dependency, workflow, secret, permission, or supply-chain change. commands.review_summary:
The PR passes a shutdown abort signal through gateway config hot reloads, drains active reload work on reloader stop, moves reloader shutdown before restart drains and channel teardown, and adds focused gateway lifecycle regression tests.

PR surface: Source +130, Tests +164. Total +294 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lets a deferred channel hot reload resume into channel stop/start without shutdown cancellation, and the linked issue provides a concrete EADDRINUSE log timeline. I did not run a live Telegram webhook reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer selection among related candidate PRs; there is no narrow automated repair left in this review.

target.title: fix #79487: Channel-reload race: deferred reload + gateway-tool `restart` double-spawn telegram channel → EADDRINUSE crash loop
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79487.

This change prevents a stale deferred channel hot reload from continuing after gateway shutdown/restart has begun. It keeps the fix at the gateway lifecycle boundary rather than adding a Telegram-specific port/start workaround:

- `startGatewayConfigReloader.stop()` now aborts and awaits an active hot-reload pass, not just future timers/watchers.
- Deferred channel reload waits observe the shutdown abort signal and exit without stop/start when the reload is superseded by restart.
- Gateway close stops the config reloader before restart active-session drains and before channel runtimes are torn down.

## What Problem This Solves

A channel config hot reload can defer Telegram stop/start while embedded gateway work is active. If `gateway-tool restart` / in-process restart begins during that wait, shutdown can drain the active work and allow the stale reload to resume while the restart lifecycle is also tearing down and starting channel runtimes. For webhook-bound Telegram, that can double-bind the same listener port and produce the `EADDRINUSE` crash loop described in #79487.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The config reloader lifecycle stopped future scheduling but did not cancel and await an already-active hot-reload pass. Channel reload deferral waits for active gateway work to drain, and restart shutdown drains exactly that work; because the active reload was not owned by shutdown, an old reload from the pre-shutdown lifecycle could resume after restart had started channel teardown/startup.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Restart supersedes any pending config hot reload, so shutdown must own and drain the config-reload lifecycle before it drains active sessions or tears down channels. This patch changes that source lifecycle invariant: `configReloader.stop()` aborts and awaits active reload work, deferred channel waits honor the abort signal, and gateway close stops the reloader before restart/session/channel shutdown steps can release stale deferred reload work.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix for gateway reload/shutdown lifecycle ordering and cancellation.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the fix is scoped to the lifecycle owner, has source and regression-test coverage, and has been reverified after merging current `origin/main`.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected from cancellation/control-flow mechanics: `catch` is used to drain a cancelled active reload during shutdown and to clean up a test promise; `return false` / `return true` make hot-reload cancellation explicit to callers. These are not symptom-masking fallbacks because the cancelled path exits only when shutdown/restart aborts the old reload lifecycle.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical source-of-truth boundary is the gateway config reload lifecycle and gateway close/restart ordering. This does not change public config schema, protocol, channel settings, provider routing, migration/default behavior, or downstream Telegram port binding; it changes when stale reload work is cancelled relative to restart.
- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope and not changed: Telegram channel config shape, webhook port allocation, channel start semantics during healthy hot reload, provider/plugin APIs, public config schema/defaults, migrations, lockfiles, and unrelated gateway behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Deferred channel reload plus restart could let a stale pre-shutdown reload resume and stop/start Telegram while restart was also restarting channels, causing a duplicate webhook listener bind and `EADDRINUSE` loop.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Operators can still hot-reload channel config while the gateway is healthy. During shutdown/restart, pending deferred channel reload work is cancelled because the full restart applies the next config through the new lifecycle, preventing duplicate Telegram webhook listener starts in the same process.
- **Real environment tested:** Source-level gateway lifecycle tests in the OpenClaw task worktree after merging current `origin/main`. Local runtime used Node v22.17.0; `pnpm` reported the repository engine warning (`wanted: >=22.19.0`) before the typecheck/lint commands passed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `pnpm lint --threads=8`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused gateway regression suite, test typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace check all passed on final HEAD after syncing with current `origin/main`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Stopping the config reloader aborts and drains an active hot reload; gateway restart stops the config reloader before active-session drains and before channel teardown; a deferred channel hot reload cancelled by shutdown does not stop/start Telegram and reports cancellation.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram webhook reproduction was not run. The race is covered with targeted lifecycle regression tests around config reload cancellation, channel reload deferral, and gateway close/restart ordering.

## Evidence

Commands run from the task worktree after merging current `origin/main`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts`
  - Result: passed (`focused_vitest_exit=0`)
- `pnpm check:test-types`
  - Result: passed (`test_types_exit=0`)
  - Note: local Node is v22.17.0, so pnpm printed the repo engine warning (`wanted: >=22.19.0`) before passing.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
  - Result: passed (`lint_exit=0`)
  - Note: local Node is v22.17.0, so pnpm printed the repo engine warning (`wanted: >=22.19.0`) before passing.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - Result: passed (`diff_check_exit=0`)

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-close.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A hot reload is active or deferred while shutdown/restart begins; shutdown aborts the old reload lifecycle before active-session drains/channel teardown can allow stale channel stop/start to resume.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests exercise the lifecycle boundaries that caused the race instead of asserting Telegram port errors after the fact: config reloader stop drains active reload, gateway close ordering stops the reloader first, and cancelled deferred Telegram reload does not call channel stop/start.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** Availability and compatibility risk labels were considered because this touches gateway reload/restart lifecycle code and config reload internals.
- **Risk explanation:** Availability risk is limited to shutdown/restart cancellation of stale reload work; normal healthy hot reload still applies. Compatibility/default behavior is unchanged: no public config schema, protocol, migration, channel option, or default is changed. Related open PR scan/background: issue #79487 had older related open PR attempts, but this PR keeps the same-contract scope focused on cancellation/drain plus close ordering rather than adding a channel-specific workaround.
- **Why acceptable:** Restart is the owner lifecycle once shutdown begins and will apply the next config through the new gateway instance. Letting an old deferred reload complete during shutdown is unnecessary and unsafe, while cancelling it removes the duplicate-start race without widening public APIs or changing normal channel hot reload semantics.
target.author: mikasa0818
